3	Stefan Georgi
MODULE 2	Research Example - Real Estate Agent Roman Alvarado
Research
EXAMPLE
Real Estate Agent Roman Alvarado
(00:00:01)
Hey guys. Roman here and I’m going to go through the research components
of the RMBC method for real estate agents. That means if you’re a real estate
agent looking for new clients, then this is going to be extremely helpful for you
with respect to knowing where to go to find all sorts of information for your
clients, what their wants are, what their needs are, what their hesitations, pain
points, victories, all those things are.
(00:00:29)
I’m sure Stefan has gone through all of the reasons why this research document
is so important, why filling it out is important and why going through each one
of these questions is so important. So I’m just going to quickly go over the
goals of what we’re trying to accomplish. We want to sell more houses using
direct mail and I’d like to differentiate that. For the purposes of this exercise,
I’m going to focus on home sellers as opposed to home buyers and I would
say, you could choose either/or and then just do it again for the other side.
There’s no reason to not to do that, but there is a very strong reason to pick
one or the other and just do it for that person and that’s because they want
different things, for sure. So home sellers.
(00:01:26)
I think the secondary goal of this is so that you could use this information for
all sorts; marketing across the board. If you’re building a website and you’re
trying to figure out what to put on that website, come back to this research
document. If you’re sending emails and you don’t know where to start, go
through this research document to pick out an attention-grabbing headline.
All sorts of things like magazine ads, things like that and lead magnets are
pretty important. I’ll even mention some ways that I could turn some of this
stuff into lead magnets because I did this before I started the video just to see
what I could find and there’s some good stuff out there.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 23 --

4	Stefan Georgi
MODULE 2	Research Example - Real Estate Agent Roman Alvarado
(00:02:12)
Then of course, just to repeat some of what Stefan has probably said, it’s really
just to get inside your prospect’s head in copywriting where just speaking to
one person, where grabbing their attention, where meeting them where they
are and so, we have to figure out where that is. To see what kind of language
they’re using online and what they’re searching for is especially important.
(00:02:39)
On that note, I am actually going to start by pretending I am a home seller and
I’m searching for something and I want to explain why that is. I’m not going to
go through each one of these questions line by line and try and answer these
things and I’m certainly not going to Google what are the hopes and dreams
of a home seller. Part of doing this correctly is knowing what questions to ask
and knowing where to find this information. Stefan’s mentioned that forums,
Amazon, Facebook and things like that work but you’ll find, as you go through
this for home sellers and homebuyers, that there are certain places where
you’re just going to live for a while because you find some golden nuggets and
that’s where it is, that’s where your people are.
(00:03:34)
So let’s get started. I’m going to just start all the way at the beginning so you
can see exactly what I did with a blank slate. What I do is I pretend I am a
home seller. If I’m a home seller, I am either beginning my journey and I want
to search for things like should I use a real estate agent to sell my house, fine.
However, what I find very helpful is let’s say the home seller has chosen a real
estate agent already. That journey could end up very badly or very good for
them; heaven and hell. So what would be a hell question? Can’t sell my house.
A heaven question would be: sold over asking. Those are my previous search
terms showing up. Cool, so I’ve entered that into Google.
(00:04:47)
I’ve already installed my Discussions button here, I’m going to activate it for
the home sellers as well. Discussions button of course, being the excellent
Chrome extension that Jimmy Parent shared a while back and you could find
it yourself simply by Googling Chrome extension, discussions and you’ll be
able to add it and it’ll show up right there. When you press it, what ends up
happening is that search term that I tried, it’s going to turn that into a search
term within the realm of forums. So that’s a forum, that’s a forum. I can’t sell
my house, help! Oopsie daisies. That’s a forum. I need to sell my house ASAP.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

(00:05:45)
Well, I’m not going to open more than three because we’re going to try and get

(00:06:19)
Cool. So let’s start with this first one here. “Hi, this is my first post so please

market for almost three weeks and EA assured us” - expert agent I’m thinking,
I don’t know - “we’d have 15 views and 10 offers within the first four weeks. I
appreciate that is sales patter, but what we’ve had is very disappointing. We’ve
had 6 views, no one requested a second viewing and no offers. Feedback
has been limited. The EA has sold lots of houses recently, so we believe it’s

interested in any feedback - the good, the bad, the ugly. The media tells us

UK. Okay, but no problem.
(00:07:12)
This is a real problem; this is something that’s going to happen to home sellers
whether they’re in Manchester or in the States or in Canada. This is a very,
very real problem. Separate that. Victory, failure and then what I’d like to do
is I just add just a header, just something for me to be able to know what that
particular blurb was all about.
(00:08:08)
I think the important thing here is expectations, like distrust. “It has been on the
market for almost three weeks and the agent” - I’m sure they use something
else in the UK instead of listing agent so that’s probably what that stands for
- “assured us, we’d have 15 views and 10 offers within the first four weeks.”
It’s almost like unmet expectations.
(00:08:50)
What are the replies? Are they all here? Okay, cool. This is good advice, “Push
them for feedback and talk to other agents, perhaps to line up a different agent
when your contract with the current agent ends.” So feedback is important.

The “listing says double drive, but there’s no picture of it and no clue where

(00:10:44)
Let’s see what this other one says. Mr. Money Mustache. “So I’ve listed back
in Iowa several weeks ago, beautiful house, nice location, all that listed for
$163K, no showings. Listed for $158K, no showings. WTF.” The problem there
is no showings and you might already be thinking in your head well, obviously
they listed too high. So this could be used as a reason why you want to use
comparative market analysis from a professional realtor to set the correct
listing price because even if you drop the price later, there’s no guarantees
that you will get that goodwill back. There’s nothing like coming out to the
market with a strong offer.
(00:11:59)
I’m going to leave this one because I know Mr. Money Mustache is a personal

too because if you’re not targeting investors who are selling their house, then
this is secondary stuff, but maybe you are. This is a pain point that somebody
has mentioned and has taken the time to express and share online, which
means if you’re the one that comes out and says, “I have a solution for this,”
you might get this guy.

Cool. So we’re going to continue on. Oh right, sorry. So let me go back to this

within the forum, this is my second entry point. Actually, there’s three then
because if this is second, then the third one is still to Google best forums for
home sellers. This second entry point is actually pretty spectacular because
if I find the actual forum homepage, I can then search for the real estate

Cool. So let’s see what we got, “Warning, this is a rant, not really a question.

“We have bought and sold a few properties so far, not investments, just

This guy’s going on for a while. “We were going back and forth with the buyer

want there just to remind you oh, that’s what this pain point is or make it more

this yourself, I mean you lose trust immediately and we’re talking about a big

How many replies that we have here? Eleven. I would go through each one of

So that’s what she’s done here. She did all this stuff, “two full bath cape, family

(00:24:34)
“We listed it for 27 days for $325K, we had four showings in the first weekend,
then not a peep. So we dropped it down to” this. Nothing and that’s just scary,
that’s the worst feeling in the world. “We’ve not sold a home in 29 years, so
this is like starting all over again. I know it’s a slow time of the year. I get that.
But how far down realistically do we go as to not show we are desperate to
leave here?” Great question, great question. That is the thing that is keeping
somebody up at night. Do I drop the price again or risk leaving it on the market
at the current price and not sell?
(00:25:20)
That stuff is the kind of - because you know why, because it’s something
that is dependent on an action that you need to take. It’s not dependent on
something external like, “I’m trapped in my house right now because there’s
a pandemic going on outside,” but I’m okay with that because I don’t want
to spread the pandemic and it’s really nothing I could do about it. So you
might as well just accept it. But if I know that I can wake up tomorrow and
make a decision that will essentially either put money in my pocket, protect
my family and put this behind us so we can move on. If I know that that’s in
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

12	Stefan Georgi
MODULE 2	Research Example - Real Estate Agent Roman Alvarado
for example, if you’re trying to lose weight. Losing weight happens gradually.
First, you lose 5 pounds, then 10 pounds, 15, 20 pounds and if you’re within a
community, you’re going to share your wins as they go along because small
wins lead to big wins. So you’ll see a lot more victories in other spaces. In real
estate., not so much because it feels like you’re just bragging at that point
like, “Hey, I just made $100,000.” Look at all this ridiculous equity.
(00:35:07)
What I like to do instead is very easy and you can literally just start going down
and unmet expectations, three weeks: sold for what my agent predicted. No
feedback from buyers would be proper feedback from every buyer. Incorrect
listing details, accurate listing details and no showings, tons of showings
and you would spell that out by essentially getting into the details of what
showings can do for you. Upgrades would be essentially spent money on the
essential upgrades and not a cent more. Realtor value would be realtor paid
for himself or herself. Excellent marketing, excellent CMA is provided and so
you can see how these are giving you a great start with respect to what you
could use. What you need to know before hiring a listing agent... or emails,
how listing mistakes can cost you thousands of dollars, stuff like that.
(00:37:57)
You’re already basically drawing a lot of inspiration from this and then I would
literally just go into some of these and pull out stories of specific examples
of what that nightmare situation looks like and you would then finish it after
giving the nightmare situation with the solution; how you would turn that
around or prevent that for somebody. But of course, this is the R of the RMBC.
There’s a reason why there’s an M and a B and a C, so these are just like things
that you just keep in mind. That’s a topic but as you proceed with the process

(00:43:04)
Let’s start there, start with the 5 stars and like Stefan said, it’s 5 stars and 1

“If you’re going to the market and selling your house without a realtor, this is
the book for this task. Even if you decide to go with a broker, the checklists in
this is slim and useful guide will help you become an informed and prepared
seller. Although, many things in this list seem obvious, there are many eureka
moments that this book would be worth three times the price.
(00:44:48)
For example, get an appraisal after house is spiffed up and you think it’s ready
for sale. This will give you a realistic assessment of what your house is really
worth in today’s market. Have you done everything in the preparation lists
like cleaning the windows or removing all of your personal crap? Have you
